# REGIME READ — 2026-06-22

## Changes Since Previous Read
- **Stage: 2 → 2 (HELD).** VIX 17.32 still inside Stage-2 band (12-18); no fresh breakdown or breakout signal over the Jun 19 (quad-witching + MOU signing) → Jun 22 first-post-event tape transition. Confidence held Medium.
- **Total score: +2 → +2 (delta 0).** No lens flipped direction. 10Y eased -3.6 bp (4.487 → 4.451), Brent eased -$0.73 (79.73 → 79.00), Gold firmed +1.24% (4,170.90 → 4,222.50), DXY firmed +0.12 (100.77 → 100.89), VIX +0.22 (17.10 → 17.32). All movements modest — no lens-changing magnitudes.
- **Triggers from previous read:**
  - **Bullish — TQQQ above $80.13:** **HELD-FIRED.** Estimated spot ~$83.5 (Jun 18 close approximation, Jun 19/22 not retrievable). No bearish reversal indicated by VIX path.
  - **Bullish — VXN crush through 22:** **ARMED.** VXN held at 26.31 (Jun 18 stale; no fresh print). 4.3 pts still to trigger.
  - **Bullish — VIX below 15 for 2+ sessions:** **ARMED.** VIX 17.32 — still in band, has not pushed lower.
  - **Bullish — F&G above 55 (Stage 1) / 45 (intermediate):** **ARMED, unmeasured.**
  - **Bullish — Clean MOU signing Jun 19:** **FIRED.** No walk-back, re-engagement, or Lebanon-clause complication headline over the weekend Jun 20-21 or into Jun 22 open. Geopolitical lens stays +2.
  - **Bullish — DJI leadership rejoining:** **ARMED, unmeasured for Jun 19/22.**
  - **Bullish — Benign PCE Jun 26:** **ARMED — pending Friday print.**
  - **Bearish — TQQQ below 50DMA $76.39:** **CLEARED** (cushion ~+9.3% from Jun 18 estimate).
  - **Bearish — VIX sustained above 22:** **CLEARED.**
  - **Bearish — VXN re-spike above 30:** **CLEARED.**
  - **Bearish — HY OAS through 300 bp:** **NOT FIRED** (no fresh print; last 278 bp).
  - **Bearish — MOU complication / Trump tariff escalation:** **CLEARED.**
- **Notable lens flips:** None. The Jun 22 read is a "no fresh information, hold" tape — the post-quad/post-MOU transition was clean enough not to demote, but no incremental data has arrived to promote toward Stage 1.

> **Snapshot freshness note:** Index closes are Jun 18 (Jun 19 close not in snapshot); VIX/DXY/Brent/Gold are Jun 22 intraday; VXN is stale Jun 18; 10Y is Jun 18. This is a **partial Monday read** — the post-MOU Friday tape and the Monday cash close are not yet recorded here.

## Regime Stage Tracker
- **Current Stage: 2 — Mild bullish/grind** (held from Jun 19 promotion).
- **Stage Anchor:** Jun 18, 2026 — broad-front recovery + VXN release. **Day 3 of Stage 2** (Jun 18 → 19 → 22, weekend excluded for trading-day count; Jun 22 is the second cash session inside Stage 2).
- **Position:** Inside first 5 sessions of Stage 2. Stage-2 durability requires either VXN sub-22 confirmation, a benign PCE Jun 26, or both. Pre-PCE drift week ahead.
- **Confidence: Medium.** Promotion holds because VIX has stayed in band Jun 18 → Jun 22 (17.10 → 17.32, a modest noise uptick, not a breakout). Reservations carried forward: VXN still 4.3 pts above durable threshold; narrow Jun 18 leadership (DJI +0.14% only) not yet resolved by fresh Jun 19/22 breadth data; F&G not reconfirmed.
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 1):** VIX sustained below 15 for 2+ sessions AND VXN below 22 AND TQQQ daily close above $88.09 (4-wk/52-wk high; also activates RALLY structure) AND F&G above 55 AND benign PCE Jun 26 AND DJI leadership rejoining.
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 3 demotion):** VIX daily close above 20 OR VXN re-spike above 30 OR TQQQ daily close below 50DMA $76.39 OR hot PCE Jun 26 (core PCE > 3.0% YoY or hawkish reaction) OR HY OAS through 300 bp OR MOU walk-back / Trump tariff escalation OR hawkish surprise from Fed-speak this week.
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 4 bypass):** Concurrent VIX above 22 + credit widening + breadth collapse.

## Regime Verdict
US risk environment **holds at Stage 2 mild-bullish on no-flip Monday tape** — VIX stays in band, geopolitical execution remained clean over the weekend, cross-asset relief intact; PCE Jun 26 is now the proximate macro test that will decide whether Stage 2 extends toward Stage 1 candidacy or falls back to Stage 3. Medium confidence.

VIX-VXN gap still ~9.0 pts. Held +1 — no fresh VXN crush confirmation but no re-spike either |

## Diverging Signals
- **Gold +1.24% vs Brent -1.06% over the Jun 19 → Jun 22 transition.** Brent falling on relief is consistent with the MOU narrative. But Gold firming back is **not** consistent with pure risk-on — modest tail-hedge demand is returning, possibly into PCE Jun 26. Worth watching: if Gold extends higher this week while VIX holds in band, that's a quiet repositioning signal that contradicts the surface-level rally tape.
- **10Y -3.6 bp + DXY +0.12 is internally inconsistent.** Yields easing should pressure the dollar; both improving slightly together suggests the bond move is a duration bid (haven flow into Treasuries) rather than rate-path repricing. Marginal — but consistent with the Gold uptick — small "quiet hedging" undercurrent in a tape that surface-reads as risk-on.
- **VIX +5.61% on a no-news Monday.** Modest, but the post-quad-witching Monday typically sees VIX *fall* (expiry-related vol unwinds), not rise. A small Monday vol bid combined with Gold firming + 10Y bid + DXY firming could be early positioning into PCE Jun 26 — descriptive note only, not a lens flip (magnitudes are small).
- **Six-of-seven lenses neutral-to-bullish vs Macro -2 persistence unchanged.** The diverging-signal structure from the Jun 19 read carries forward — no resolution on the bond-market gate.

## TQQQ Read
- **Momentum:** RSI estimated ~65-70 — upper-neutral / approaching overbought. Two-day Jun 16-17 down streak broken Jun 18. No fresh Jun 19/22 RSI data; absent a sharp Jun 19 reversal, the reading should hold.
- **Market structure: SIDEWAYS (held).** Estimated -5.2% from 4-wk high $88.09 (well above -20% DOWNTURN threshold) AND +22.6% above 4-wk low $68.08 (still below +30% RALLY threshold = $88.50). RALLY activation needs a close ≥ $88.50, which is +6.0% from estimated spot — well within a one-sigma move. DOWNTURN no longer proximate (needs ≤ $70.47 + VXN ≥ 25, ~15.6% drop).
- **Trend:** Price estimated above both 50DMA ($76.39, cushion ~+9.3%) and 200DMA ($68.09, cushion ~+22.6%). Both MAs supportive backdrops, not proximate threats.
- **Volatility & premium environment:** **VXN held at 26.31 (no fresh Monday print in snapshot)** — the Jun 18 NDX-vol release has not been confirmed-or-denied by a Jun 19/22 update. Est. TQQQ IV ~78.9% (held). EM ±11.1% (held). Premium remains elevated relative to pre-FOMC norm but is no longer event-fattened on Jun 18 evidence; whether the release sticks through the week is the key unknown. **VIX +5.61% Monday is a modest re-firming**, suggesting the broad-vol crush isn't extending — flag for tomorrow's read.
- **Put-selling context: FAVOURABLE (held).** Holds for the same reasons as Jun 19: bullish triggers fired (TQQQ above gate, MOU clean, 50DMA cushion expanded), structural risk events cleared (quad-witching past, MOU executed), and vol overhang has begun releasing on Jun 18 evidence. **New reservations vs Jun 19:** (a) **VIX modestly re-firming on a no-news Monday** is not the path Stage 2 expects — would want to see VIX drift lower into PCE, not bid; (b) **Gold +1.24% + 10Y -3.6 bp + DXY +0.12 cluster** is a quiet tail-hedge undercurrent that contradicts the surface relief tape; (c) **EM lower bound $74.2 still sits below 50DMA $76.39** — a one-sigma decline tests the bullish gate; (d) PCE Jun 26 now inside the 5-day window — event risk is the proximate decider. **Net: FAVOURABLE held, but it's a Stage-2 favourable with PCE-Jun-26 event risk inside the window and small quiet-hedging undercurrents to monitor.** Macro lens still -2; F&G not reconfirmed. Context only — no strikes, sizes, expiries, or trade calls.

## Forward Levels & Triggers
- **Bullish trigger (→ Stage 1):** TQQQ daily close above $88.09 (4-wk/52-wk high — also activates RALLY structure) AND VXN sustains below 22 AND VIX below 15 for 2+ sessions AND F&G reclaims 55 AND DJI leadership rejoining AND benign PCE Jun 26 (core PCE in-line or cooler + dovish-or-neutral market reaction).
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 3 demotion):** TQQQ daily close below 50DMA $76.39, OR VXN re-spike above 30, OR VIX daily close above 20, OR hot PCE Jun 26 (core PCE > 3.0% YoY or hawkish reaction), OR HY OAS through 300 bp, OR MOU walk-back / Trump tariff escalation, OR hawkish surprise from Fed-speak this week.
- **Bearish trigger (→ Stage 4 bypass):** Concurrent VIX above 22 + credit widening + breadth collapse.
- **Neutralizer:** A clean Jun 22-25 drift week with VIX easing back below 15, VXN finally breaking 22, fresh Jun 19/22 close data confirming 50DMA cushion, and a benign PCE Jun 26 — would lift Stage-2 toward Stage-1 candidacy. The current Monday tape is **insufficient** to trigger that on its own.

## This Period's Catalysts
- **Jun 23-25, 2026 (Tue-Thu):** Fed-speak likely (post-FOMC blackout window has lifted); Micron (MU) earnings (date to confirm — typically week of Jun 22-26).
- **Jun 26, 2026 (Fri):** **PCE Inflation (May reading)** — primary Fed-preferred print; **inside the 5-session window from today**. (Note: Jun 27 is Saturday — release is Jun 26 Friday.)
- **Jun 30, 2026 (Tue):** Quarter-end rebalance — outsized close-auction activity; ~6 trading days out.
- **Jul 3, 2026 (Fri):** NFP June payrolls — pre-July-4 close.

## Expiry-Window Flag
- **Catalysts inside the next 5 trading days (Jun 22 today, Jun 23, Jun 24, Jun 25, Jun 26):** Fed-speak likely Jun 23-25; MU earnings likely Jun 23-26; **PCE Jun 26 Friday**.
- **Window: EVENT-WINDOW** — PCE Jun 26 is the binding macro decision event inside the window. (Quad-witching Jun 19 is now past; MOU signing Jun 19 is now past; both cleared cleanly.)

## Structural Calendar Flag (deterministic — always report)
- **Quad-witching:** Last was **Jun 19, 2026** (passed). Next is **Sep 18, 2026** (~63 trading days out). Outside relevance window.
- **Quarter-end rebalance:** Next is **Jun 30, 2026** (last business day of Q2). Days-until: **~6 trading days.** Outside 2-session window today; flag again Jun 25-26 as it approaches. Structurally elevated close-auction activity Jun 30 + likely month-end rebalancing drift Jun 26-30 are normal.
- **Net:** **No structural calendar event inside the 2-session window.** Path Jun 22-25 is structurally normal until PCE Jun 26. Quarter-end Jun 30 is approaching but not yet active. Descriptive only — no trade calls.

## EVENT ALERT — Historical Playbook
- **PCE Inflation (May reading) — Jun 26, 2026 (Fri, inside the 5-session window).** Historical anchors for PCE prints in a comparable regime (sticky-inflation overlay + hawkish-Fed-dots reset + risk-on vol-crush in flight): **Apr 28, 2023 PCE** (core PCE 4.6% YoY in-line during early-2023 disinflation tape — SPX +0.8% on the day, VIX -1.0 pt, mild relief follow-through into next week); **Jun 30, 2023 PCE** (core 4.1% YoY, cooler-than-expected — SPX +1.2%, VIX -1.4 pt, durable risk-on continuation); **May 31, 2024 PCE** (core 2.8% YoY in-line — SPX -0.6% in pre-event positioning but +0.7% on print, VIX -0.8 pt, mild relief); **Jun 28, 2024 PCE** (core 2.6% YoY in-line — SPX +0.1%, VIX -0.3 pt, muted). Pattern across **in-line PCE prints during sticky-but-improving inflation regimes:** modest pre-event vol-bid (VIX +0.3-0.8 pt the day before), then post-print VIX crush of 0.5-1.5 pts, SPX flat-to-+1.2% on the day, follow-through into next week typically positive when broader regime is risk-on. **Hot-print episodes in comparable regimes:** **Feb 24, 2023 PCE** (core 4.7% YoY hot — SPX -1.1%, VIX +1.1 pt); **Mar 31, 2023 PCE** (core 4.6% YoY hot — SPX -1.4% morning then recovered to -0.2%, VIX +0.8 pt then crush). Pattern: **hot prints cost ~1-2% on SPX + 1-2 pt VIX spike on the day**, often partially retraced by close as dealers re-position. NDX historically moves ~1.3× SPX into and out of PCE prints in vol-elevated regimes.
- **Net alert:** Carrying short-put exposure on TQQQ through PCE Jun 26 means accepting: (1) **Pre-PCE vol-bid** is likely Jun 24-25 if historical pattern holds — VIX could re-firm from 17.32 toward 18-19 even with no fresh negative news (this may already be visible in today's VIX +5.61%); (2) **Print-day asymmetry** — in-line/cool PCE typically gives modest VIX-crush + SPX-up tape; hot PCE costs 1-2% on SPX, ~3-6% on TQQQ on 3× leverage, with VIX spike of 1-2 pts that would partially repackage TQQQ premium back up; (3) **NDX historically moves ~1.3× SPX into PCE in vol-elevated regimes** — TQQQ on 3× leverage = ~4× SPX-PCE-day move; (4) **Persistent macro headwind** — even an in-line PCE doesn't unwind the FOMC dot reset (3.8% terminal), it only takes the immediate hawkish-surprise risk off the table; (5) **Stage 2 promotion is conditional on a benign PCE** — a hot print very likely triggers Stage-3 demotion per the trigger list above. **Historical post-PCE path for in-line/cool prints in comparable regimes:** typical 3-5 sessions of constructive drift into the next macro catalyst (NFP Jul 3). **For hot prints:** 2-3 sessions of risk-off retest of recent lows, with the 50DMA gate ($76.39) becoming the deciding level for TQQQ. **Descriptive only — no trade calls.**

> **Hard-rules compliance notes:**
> - Analysis only. No structures, strikes, expiries, or sizing anywhere.
> - **Jun 22 is a partial Monday snapshot:** indices = Jun 18 closes (Jun 19/22 closes not in snapshot block); VIX/DXY/Brent/Gold = Jun 22 intraday; VXN/10Y = Jun 18 close. Marked as carry-forward where Jun 19/22 data not retrievable.
> - TQQQ Jun 18 close not retrievable — ~$83.5 marked clearly as 3× NDX approximation; Jun 19/22 quotes explicitly not in snapshot.
> - VIX 17.32, VXN 26.31, DXY 100.89, Brent 79.00, Gold 4,222.50, 10Y 4.451 from live snapshot block — used verbatim.
> - **Key Jun 22 update is HOLD** — no lens flipped; the post-quad/post-MOU transition was clean; PCE Jun 26 is the proximate event-window decider; small quiet-hedging undercurrent (Gold + 10Y + Monday VIX bid) flagged but not lens-changing.
> - **PCE date correction:** Jun 27 (per Jun 19 prior read) is Saturday; actual release is Jun 26 Friday. Window math updated accordingly.
> - Conservative scoring: all lenses held at prior levels; nothing promoted on a no-fresh-data Monday.
> - **2s10s** estimated +24 bp (10Y 4.451 − 2Y ~4.21) — slight intra-week flattening from prior ~+28 bp; labeled estimate.
